The Languas genus, also known as the finger root or lesser galangal, is a type of rhizome-producing plant native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help alleviate digestive issues and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Languas suishaensis, a hypothetical species, may include compounds such as gingerols, shogaols, and paradols, which are known for their medicinal properties.
